channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 2 hours.

item.title: 📍 Trump’s Southeast Asia Trade Deals Are in Limbo | Foreign Policy
item.description: Malaysia becomes the first country to cancel its tariff deal with the White House.

item.title: 🌏 US Army fires Tomahawk missile from new Typhon launcher during Philippines drill | Defense News
item.description: “The missile was very precise. Let’s say if you want to hit a window from a far distance, it lands directly into that window,” a military official said.

item.title: 🏛️ Acquisition Reform Needs Its Own Wargame | War on the Rocks
item.description: One number buried in the Pentagon’s Fiscal Year 2027 budget request reveals a decade of acquisition decisions in a data point: The U.S. Navy is requesting 785 Tomahawk cruise missiles. In 2025, Congress funded 55. That 1,200 percent jump is the cost of choices never…

item.title: ⚛️ Practice Makes Deterrence: India’s Next Nuclear Challenge at Sea | War on the Rocks
item.description: When India’s third ballistic missile-carrying, nuclear-powered submarine — the INS Aridhaman — entered service on April 3, 2026, it marked a milestone decades in the making.With three boomers, India can now reliably always maintain at least one on deterrent patrol, thus…

item.title: 🌍 Abandoned and Ungoverned: Lebanon’s Palestinian and Syrian Populations and the Emerging Radicalization Landscape | War on the Rocks
item.description: Inside Lebanon, the conditions for the next extremist uprising are quietly taking root.In the summer of 2007, the Lebanese Armed Forces fought Fatah al-Islam for three months inside Nahr al-Bared, a Palestinian refugee camp in northern Lebanon. Fatah al-Islam was a…

item.title: 📍 Australia’s AI moment requires a copyright compromise | ASPI Strategist
item.description: I’ve just finished reading a fantasy novel written entirely by an AI. The Second Son of the House of Bells is the story of a young bellmaker whose keen musical ear makes him an agent of political ...

item.title: 📍 BJP Makes Major Gains in 3 of 5 State Assembly Elections | The Diplomat
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi's party has stormed to power in West Bengal and retained its hold over Assam and Puducherry.

item.title: 📍 The ICJ and the Limits of International Justice For the Rohingya | The Diplomat
item.description: International courts can apportion legal responsibility, but cannot guarantee justice, safety, or safe return to Myanmar for more than 1 million Rohingya refugees.

item.title: 🌏 The Return of Japanese Hard Power | Foreign Affairs
item.description: Why Tokyo is bulking up its defense-industrial base.

item.title: 🌏 Trump’s China Trap | Foreign Affairs
item.description: Why Xi keeps winning the summitry game.

item.title: 🏛️ Only Congress Can Fix American Trade | Foreign Affairs
item.description: Trump’s tariff mess offers a chance to restore legislative oversight.

item.title: 📍 Singapore, New Zealand Agree to Keep Vital Supplies Flowing in Times of Crisis | The Diplomat
item.description: The Agreement on Trade in Essential Supplies, which was agreed in October, is the world’s first legally-binding bilateral supply chain resilience agreement.

item.title: 🏴 Zelenskyy rains on Putin’s parade: Kyiv and Moscow declare rival ceasefires | Atlantic Council
item.description: Victory Day was supposed to be an opportunity for Vladimir Putin to project power. Instead, this year’s drastically scaled down parade is exposing Russia’s weakness while revealing Ukraine's strength, writes Peter Dickinson. The post Zelenskyy rains on Putin’s parade: Kyiv and…

item.title: 🏛️ Pentagon seeks smarter, self-organizing drones as autonomous-warfare budget is poised to skyrocket | Defense One
item.description: Uncrewed weapons actually require a lot of people. New DARPA projects aim to overcome that.

item.title: 🌏 Indonesia, Japan Sign New Defense Agreement, Eye Possible Arms Transfers | The Diplomat
item.description: With the recent removal of its longstanding ban on lethal arms exports, Tokyo is set to become an active defense industry player in Southeast Asia.

item.title: 📍 In this month’s budgets, treat economic decisions as national-security decisions | ASPI Strategist
item.description: The Northern Territory still struggles to convert opportunity into sustained economic growth because workforce shortages, weak enabling infrastructure and slow approvals continue to choke investment. With the May budgets in Darwin and Canberra fast approaching, ...

item.title: 🏛️ Air Force clears T-7A Red Hawk for low-rate production | Defense News
item.description: The decision authorizes a $219 million contract for the first 14 aircraft.

item.title: 📍 Letter From The Editor: 10 Years And 11,000 Articles Later | The War Zone
item.description: TWZ was launched 10 years ago today and we are still here and growing thanks to all of you. The post Letter From The Editor: 10 Years And 11,000 Articles Later appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 Charai for The National Interest: Iran’s Terror Regime Has Shown Its True Face | Atlantic Council
item.description: The post Charai for The National Interest: Iran’s Terror Regime Has Shown Its True Face appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 First B-52s to get new engines this year | Defense One
item.description: Critical design review clears Boeing to upgrade two Stratofortresses in bid to keep them flying past 2050.

item.title: 🌍 U.S. AH-64 Apache, MH-60 Seahawk Helicopters Sink Six Iranian Boats (Updated) | The War Zone
item.description: Commercial vessels and American warships are coming under Iranian attack around the Strait of Hormuz as a new U.S. maritime protection plan has kicked off. The post U.S. AH-64 Apache, MH-60 Seahawk Helicopters Sink Six Iranian Boats (Updated) appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🕵️ NRO awards three new contracts for commercial satellite data | Breaking Defense
item.description: The head of the NRO’s commercial programs, Pete Muend, suggested that spysat agency could vet the ability of commercial satellite firms to track airborne targes for the US Space Force.

item.title: 🕵️ US military accompanies commercial carrier through Strait of Hormuz | Defense News
item.description: The shipping company Maersk said the transit of its Alliance Fairfax was completed without incident and that all crew were safe and unharmed.

item.title: 🌍 Introducing ‘Project Freedom’ | Foreign Policy
item.description: Trump’s latest gambit to reopen the Strait of Hormuz has inflamed the fragile cease-fire with Iran.

item.title: 🌍 Navy F/A-18Gs over Iran, Venezuela show rise in aerial electronic attack | Defense One
item.description: Here's what's next for the Navy jamming plane.

item.title: 🌍 Project Freedom unlikely to pay off in Strait of Hormuz right away, analysts say | Breaking Defense
item.description: CENTCOM Commander Adm. Brad Cooper told reporters today that six Iranian small boats were destroyed by US forces as the operation began.

item.title: 🏴 What Romania’s no-confidence vote really means for its European future | Atlantic Council
item.description: Romania's parliament is set to vote on a no-confidence motion against Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan's minority government. The post What Romania’s no-confidence vote really means for its European future appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🌍 Israel’s First KC-46 Tanker Is Now Flying | The War Zone
item.description: The first new tanker is set to arrive in Israel in around a month and will help relieve pressure on the IAF's tiny and very old KC-707 tanker force. The post Israel’s First KC-46 Tanker Is Now Flying appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏛️ How Hegseth, Caine, and Hurst defended their budget strategy on the Hill | Breaking Defense
item.description: Valerie Insinna has the highlights from last week’s House and Senate hearings on the budget.

item.title: ⚛️ With its third SSBN, India edges closer to continuous at-sea deterrence | ASPI Strategist
item.description: India’s commissioning of the submarine INS Aridhaman last month is significant as a strategic marker in the evolution of India’s sea-based nuclear deterrent. In adding a third boat to India’s fleet of nuclear-powered ballistic missile ...
